question_html: mgvavb Ki: sin θ+sin 2θ+sin 3θ=1+cos θ+cos 2θ |
solution_html:
sin θ+sin 2θ+sin 3θ=1+cos θ+cos 2θ

⇒2sin 2θcos θ+sin 2θ=2cos2 θ+cos θ

⇒sin 2θ(2cos θ+1)=cos θ(2cos θ+1)

⇒sin 2θ(2cos θ+1)−cos θ(2cos θ+1)=0

⇒(2cos θ+1)(sin 2θ−cos θ)=0

⇒2cos θ+1=0⇒cos θ=−12

⇒cos θ=cos 2π3 ∴θ=2nπ±2π3

A_ev, sin 2θ−cos θ=0

⇒2sin θcos θ−cos θ=0

⇒cos θ(2sin θ−1)=0 

∴cos θ=0 ∴θ=(2n+1)π2

A_ev, 2sin θ−1=0⇒2sin θ=1⇒sin θ=12 

⇒sin θ=sin π6 ∴θ=nπ+(−1)nπ6

∴ wb‡Y©q mgvavb: θ=2nπ±2π3

question_html:
Read the following sentences and change them as directed in the bracket:

(a) On hearing the news of his failure, he broke down in tears. (Complex)
‎(b) This is the most inspiring story I have ever read in my life. (Positive)
‎(c) The committee members will announce the final decision at the end of the meeting. (Passive)
‎(d) Education is the key to solving many of the problems in our society. (Interrogative)
‎(e) Eve-teasing creates fear and insecurity among young girls in public places. (Negative)
solution_html:
(a) He broke down in tears when he heard the news of his failure./ When he heard the news of his failure, he broke down in tears.
‎(b) No other story that I have read in my life is as inspiring as this one.
‎(c) The final decision will be announced by the committee members at the end of the meeting.
‎(d) Isn’t education the key to solving many of the problems in our society?
‎(e) Eve-teasing does not create safety and confidence among young girls in public places./ Eve-teasing does not create anything but fear and insecurity among young girls in public places.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for changing the sentences correctly.

question_html:
Make meaningful sentences using the following idioms:

(a) A bolt from the blue

(b) Black and white

(c) Bite the bullet

(d) Carry coals to Newcastle

(e) Let the cat out of the bag
solution_html:
(a) A bolt from the blue – Her visit was a bolt from the blue.

(b) Black and white – The rules are in black and white.

(c) Bite the bullet – Even though he was scared, he decided to bite the bullet and face the exam.
‎(d) Carry coals to Newcastle – Bringing bread was like carrying coals to Newcastle.

(e) Let the cat out of the bag – He accidentally let the cat out of the bag about the surprise party.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for making meaningful sentences using the idioms.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Floods and Their Impact on Bangladesh’.
solution_html:
‘Floods and Their Impact on Bangladesh’

Floods are one of the most common and devastating natural disasters in Bangladesh. The country’s flat and low-lying geography makes it extremely prone to flooding, especially during the monsoon season. Heavy rainfall combined with overflowing rivers causes widespread waterlogging and submergence of vast areas. These floods damage homes, destroy crops, and disrupt transportation and communication. Many people lose their livelihoods as agricultural lands become unusable for months. Health risks rise sharply due to waterborne diseases and lack of clean drinking water. Despite government efforts to build embankments and improve drainage, floods continue to cause immense hardship. The increasing intensity of floods is linked to climate change, adding more pressure on the vulnerable population. Effective flood management, community awareness, and international cooperation are crucial to lessen the impact and protect millions of people. Floods remain a formidable challenge, but with united efforts, Bangladesh can build resilience against this recurring threat.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing the title “Floods and Their Impact on Bangladesh”

01 mark for explaining Bangladesh’s geographic vulnerability and flood causes.

1.5 marks for describing the impacts of floods on homes, agriculture, and health.

01 mark for mentioning government efforts and climate change influence.

01 mark for emphasizing the need for flood management, awareness, and cooperation.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

*0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]
